sections.paragraph: with suff. בִּזָּהּ m. prey, spoil. It is used of persons and cattle carried away in war (elsewhere שְׁבִי, מַלְקֹחַ), also of wealth taken from an enemy, Num 14:3 Jer 15:13 49:32 . בָּזַז בַּז see בָּזַז . Of frequent use are the phrases הָיָה לָבַז to become a prey, to be carried away as a prey, Num 14:31 Deu 1:39 Isa 42:22 sometimes with the addition of a dative, Eze 26:5 34:28 נָתַן לָבַז to give for a prey, Jer 17:3 Eze 25:7 קרי.
